core_mechanism: nearest-neighbour local-alignment (majority-preference) dynamics on a lattice — a zero-temperature Ising-type energy minimisation — that spontaneously separates an initially mixed two-state configuration into large single-type domains (phase separation / domain coarsening)
brief_reason: The abstract casts the model as a square-lattice Ising model at zero temperature driven by neighbour preferences that produce spontaneous segregation into homogeneous domains, with an added feedback between the segregation order parameter and the 'temperature' noise level that self-organises that control parameter.
